title: Quesadilla
tagline: Crispy outside, cheesy inside — one high-protein quesadilla, two fillings: seasoned chicken strips or schezwan paneer.
servingsNote: 2 large quesadillas; the chicken filling can be batch-cooked from 500 g for meal prep
macroNotes: Estimated for the lean chicken version (2 quesadillas).
This is a strong gym meal — ~48 g protein for ~520 kcal per serving because the chicken is high and the cheese is controlled (~50 g per quesadilla, not the usual 100 g+).
Leaner still: use one tortilla open-faced instead of folded, swap in low-fat mozzarella, and use an egg-white wash instead of butter to crisp — drops ~120–150 kcal per serving.
The schezwan paneer version is vegetarian and still ~30 g protein per serving from 200 g paneer + cheese; add a Greek-yogurt-and-gherkin dip for extra protein with almost no fat.

items: 4 cheese slices (2 per quesadilla) — slices melt faster and act as a moisture barrier
1 tsp butter or oil, for crisping (or spray oil)

ingredients.group: Filling option A — seasoned chicken (per 2 quesadillas)
items: 250–300 g chicken breast, cut into thin strips (batch up to 500 g and store the rest)
1 tsp jeera (cumin) POWDER — not whole seeds
1/2–3/4 tsp red chilli powder (Kashmiri is milder; regular is stronger, use less)
1 tsp pickle/gherkin juice or a squeeze of lime, to finish
200 g paneer, crumbled or small cubes
Salt, chilli powder, pepper, to taste
A few pickled gherkins, sliced and patted dry

steps.title: Season the chicken (option A)
steps.body: Toss 250–300 g chicken strips with 1 tsp jeera POWDER, 1/2–3/4 tsp red chilli powder, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper and 1/2 tsp garlic powder. Rest 10–15 minutes. Use powdered jeera, not whole seeds — whole seeds don't distribute on thin strips and give random bitter bites. Measure the jeera carefully: a tablespoon instead of a teaspoon (3× too much) will dominate everything and taste earthy/bitter.

steps.title: Sear the chicken
steps.body: Heat a pan medium-high, add 1 tsp oil, spread the strips in a single layer and don't move them for 2 minutes. Flip once browned; total 6–8 minutes until no pink and no water pooling. If liquid appears, raise the heat until it evaporates — wet filling makes a soggy quesadilla. Off the heat, toss with 1 tsp pickle juice or lime for brightness. Rest 3–4 minutes.

steps.title: OR make schezwan paneer (option B)
steps.body: Heat a pan with 1 tbsp oil/butter, add the capsicum for 30–40 seconds, then 200 g paneer, 1–1.5 tbsp schezwan sauce and a little salt, chilli powder and pepper. Toss on medium-high until the paneer is hot and slightly crisp. Turn off the heat.

steps.title: Cook the veg
steps.body: In the same pan on medium-high with 1 tsp oil, add the onion first for 1–2 minutes, then the capsicum. Cook ~5 minutes until softened with light browning and no water in the pan; season with a small pinch of salt only after 2–3 minutes (early salt pulls water). Don't cook them to sabzi-softness.

steps.title: Prep the cold bits
steps.body: Slice the gherkins thin and pat them dry so their brine doesn't sog the quesadilla. Keep the lettuce (and carrot if using) cold and raw — don't cook them; toss lightly with a little mayo and a few drops Tabasco.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Pan on medium with a light butter/oil coating. Tortilla down, then 1 cheese slice for full coverage (glue + moisture barrier), then the hot filling and warm veg, a light scatter of gherkins, the cold lettuce last, then the second cheese slice. Fold in half.

steps.title: Crisp it
steps.body: Cook ~2–3 minutes per side, pressing gently with a spatula, flipping once golden. Remove when the cheese is fully melted.

steps.title: Rest & serve
steps.body: Let it rest 1 minute before cutting so the cheese sets. Serve with sour cream, salsa, guac or a Greek-yogurt-gherkin dip.

notes: Meal prep: brown the chicken and veg fully, then cool them uncovered on a tray 15–20 minutes before sealing (trapped steam = sog). Store chicken and veg separately, use within 24–48 h, and reheat in a pan (not the microwave) before assembling.
Don't add schezwan sauce to raw chicken before cooking — its sugar and moisture make the chicken steam and burn instead of browning. Add 1–2 tsp at the end if you want the flavour.
Grate your own cheese if using a block — pre-shredded has anti-caking starch that melts worse. But cheese slices are ideal here for a fast, clean melt.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
